title: Limit Unsuccessful Logon Attempts

checks.name: Means of Limiting Unsuccessful Logon Attempts is Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of the means for limiting unsuccessful logon attempts on Windows Client is a GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: Defined Means of Limiting Unsuccessful Logon Attempts is Implemented
checks.purpose: Verify that account lockout policy is configured with an acceptable threshold, lockout duration, and observation window via local security policy

checks.name: Means of Limiting Unsuccessful Logon Attempts is Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that account lockout policy is defined and enforced via Group Policy at the domain level with documented threshold, duration, and observation window values

checks.name: Defined Means of Limiting Unsuccessful Logon Attempts is Implemented
checks.purpose: Confirm that the domain-level account lockout policy is actively enforced with acceptable threshold, lockout duration, and observation window values

checks.name: Means of Limiting Unsuccessful Logon Attempts is Defined
checks.expected_result: deny and unlock_time values present and non-zero in faillock.conf or PAM configuration
checks.purpose: Verify that the means of limiting unsuccessful logon attempts is defined via pam_faillock or pam_tally2 configuration with explicit deny and unlock_time values

checks.name: Defined Means of Limiting Unsuccessful Logon Attempts is Implemented
checks.purpose: Confirm that pam_faillock or pam_tally2 is active in PAM stack with an acceptable deny threshold and unlock time, and that SSH MaxAuthTries is configured

checks.name: Means of Limiting Unsuccessful Logon Attempts is Defined
checks.expected_result: deny and unlock_time values present and non-zero in faillock.conf or PAM configuration
checks.purpose: Verify that the means of limiting unsuccessful logon attempts is defined via pam_faillock or pam_tally2 configuration with explicit deny and unlock_time values

checks.name: Defined Means of Limiting Unsuccessful Logon Attempts is Implemented
checks.purpose: Confirm that pam_faillock or pam_tally2 is active in PAM stack with an acceptable deny threshold and unlock time, and that SSH MaxAuthTries is configured

remediation: On Windows, configure account lockout policy via Group Policy under Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Account Lockout Policy. Set the lockout threshold to 5 or fewer invalid attempts, lockout duration to at least 15 minutes, and the reset counter after to 15 minutes. Apply this policy at the domain level for domain-joined systems. On Linux, configure pam_faillock in /etc/security/faillock.conf and ensure it is active in /etc/pam.d/common-auth with a deny threshold of 5 or fewer and an unlock time of 900 seconds or more. Also set MaxAuthTries to 4 or fewer in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.
